com.mentor.chs.api

Interface IXLibrary


  • @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXLibrary
    Interface to access library

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.util.Set<IXLibraryColorCode> getColorCodes()
      Get all the color codes in Library.

      Get all the component type codes in Library.

      Get all the customer organisations in Library.

      Get the library object representing the input part number .

      Get the set of library objects mathching the input group name and the part number as a regular expression.

      Get all the manufacturing organisations in Library.

    • Method Detail

      • getLibraryObject

        IXLibraryObject getLibraryObject(java.lang.String partNumber)
        Get the library object representing the input part number .
        Parameters:
        partNumber - Part Number of the library object (part) to be retrieved.
        Returns:
        the library object .

      • getLibraryObjects

        java.util.Set<IXLibraryObject> getLibraryObjects(java.lang.String groupName,
                                                 java.lang.String partNumber)
        Get the set of library objects mathching the input group name and the part number as a regular expression.
        Parameters:
        groupName - group name of the library objects (parts) to be retrieved. Possible group name values are "Assembly", "Backshell","Backshell Plug","Backshell Seal","Cavity Group","Cavity Plug", "Cavity Seal","Clip","Connector","Connector Seal", "Device","Fixture","Grommet","Heat Shrink Sleeve","IDC Connector","In-House Assembly","Multicore Wire","Other","Solder Sleeve","Splice","Tape","Terminal","Tube","Ultrasonic Weld","Wire"
        partNumber - PartNumber search string using which library is searched. There is a limited regular expression support for this. Supported special regex characters are * or % to specify multipe wild chars and ? or _ to specify single wild character
        Returns:
        Set of matching library objects if any, empty set otherwise. Here is the example of using this 
         Suppose library have parts with part numbers B12A1, B12B1, B12B13, B12B21, B22B1, A12B1
        lib.getLibraryObjects("Connector","B*B1");  This returns parts B12B1 and B22B1
        lib.getLibraryObjects("Connector","B*B*");  This returns parts B12B1, B12B13, B12B21, B22B1
        lib.getLibraryObjects("Connector","B%B%");  Same as previous one and returns parts B12A1, B12B1, B12B13, B12B21, B22B1
        lib.getLibraryObjects("Connector","B12?1");  This returns parts B12A1 and B12B1
        lib.getLibraryObjects("Connector","B12_1");  Same as previous one and returns parts B12A1 and B12B1
        lib.getLibraryObjects("Connector","*1_B1*");  This returns parts B12B1,B12B13 and A12B1

        Get all the color codes in Library.
